hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Production and Operations Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. Spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #22.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in Production and Operations Management covers manufacturing operations, supply chain management, quality systems, and production planning for industrial and service sector professionals. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 at ₹2,25,000. Best for plant managers, production engineers, and operations professionals targeting manufacturing leadership roles.

about.body:
Production and Operations Management is the MBA specialisation built for professionals who manage the physical and logistical core of an organisation: factories, plants, assembly lines, warehouses, and supply chains. The Amity Online MBA in Production and Operations Management provides the management layer that production-experienced engineers and operations executives often lack when moving into senior plant management, operations director, or COO roles.

This specialisation is directly relevant to India's manufacturing sector, which employs millions and is a focus of government programmes such as Make in India and PLI schemes. It covers production planning, quality systems, supply chain optimisation, and lean manufacturing applied in an Indian manufacturing context.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #22 University ranking for 2025. The degree carries the Amity University name identical to the campus degree. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from Amity. Full programme overview at /universities/amity-university-online/mba.

syllabus.note: EdifyEdu has not yet received the detailed Sem 3-4 subject list for this specialisation. Contact Amity Online directly for the current curriculum before enrolling.

whoHires.body:
Production and Operations Management MBA graduates from Amity University Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Manufacturing, Logistics, Process industries, EPC contractors, Pharmaceutical

**Entry-level roles:** Production Supervisor (management track), Operations Analyst, Plant Administration Executive, Manufacturing Planning Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Plant Manager, Production Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Quality Head, Operations Head

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Director of Manufacturing, COO (mid-size manufacturer), Plant Director

**Top hiring organisations:** Tata Motors, Mahindra, Bajaj Auto, Bharat Forge, L&T, Asian Paints, Nestle India, Sun Pharmaceutical

skills.body:
The Production and Operations Management specialisation builds skills that manufacturing and industrial employers test at plant manager and operations director level:

**Technical skills:**
- Production Planning: MRP and MPS systems, aggregate production planning, capacity management, and scheduling
- Quality Management: ISO 9001, Six Sigma DMAIC methodology, SPC charts, and quality audit management
- Supply Chain Management: vendor development, raw material procurement strategy, logistics optimisation, and inventory management

**Applied skills:**
- Lean Manufacturing: waste reduction, value stream mapping, Kaizen events, and OEE improvement programmes
- Operations Strategy: make-or-buy decisions, facility planning, technology investment, and capacity expansion strategy

**Soft skills:** Managing unionised shop-floor workforces, presenting OEE and quality data to plant boards, navigating multi-shift operations management.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management against peers:

**vs LPU Operations Management MBA:** LPU offers Operations Management at lower fees and NAAC A++. LPU has strong placement infrastructure specifically in manufacturing north India (Punjab, Haryana). Amity's NIRF #22 gives it an edge for MNC manufacturing roles. For candidates in the Delhi NCR manufacturing belt, both are strong choices.

**vs Chandigarh University Operations MBA:** CU is NIRF #19 and NAAC A+. CU has strong manufacturing placement connections in north India. For manufacturing roles at Indian companies outside Delhi NCR, CU is competitive. Amity has stronger pan-India and MNC brand recognition. Choose based on target employer geography and company type.

**vs Amity General Management MBA:** General Management at Amity covers Operations Planning and Scheduling as two of its 13 Sem 3-4 subjects, but within a cross-functional framework. Production and Operations Management is dedicated to manufacturing and plant operations. If your career is manufacturing-specific, this spec is more targeted. If you need cross-functional breadth, General Management is better.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Amity University Online MBA in Production and Operations Management is ₹2,25,000 across 4 semesters. Per-semester fee is ₹56,300. Amity does not charge a separate registration fee.

For working professionals, Amity offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately ₹8,906 per month through its NBFC lending partners.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Amity University Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The Production and Operations Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA Production and Operations Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Suresh Patil
items.liked: Supply chain management and lean manufacturing content was directly applicable to my auto component plant operations
items.disliked: Industry 4.0 and digital manufacturing content was lighter than expected given how fast the sector is changing
items.body: I am a production engineer managing a 400-person auto component plant. The MBA gave me the management vocabulary and operational strategy frameworks I was missing. The supply chain and quality management content improved how I structure vendor development conversations. Got promoted to Plant Manager 8 months after completing the degree. Amity NIRF #22 was specifically validated by our Group HR.

items.name: Deepak Verma
items.liked: Production planning and capacity management content was systematic — gave structure to decisions I was making ad-hoc
items.disliked: Six Sigma content was introductory — Green Belt level content would have been more useful for a practising quality manager
items.body: I manage pharmaceutical plant operations and needed a management degree to qualify for VP Operations. The operations management and quality systems content was genuinely useful. The strategy and management subjects from Sem 1-2 gave me the business context I was missing as an operations specialist. The Amity degree was accepted for the VP Operations application without question.

items.name: Rohini Kaur
items.liked: EHS management and operational compliance content was thorough — important for factory operations management
items.disliked: Logistics and last-mile delivery content was limited — relevant for manufacturers doing direct supply to retail
items.body: I manage operations at a garment export unit. The production planning and supply chain content was directly applicable to our seasonal demand cycles. The quality management frameworks helped us achieve our first ISO certification audit. The Amity degree gave me the credentials to apply for operations director roles at larger garment manufacturers in the region.

faqs.question: Is this MBA suitable for engineers without prior management experience?
faqs.answer: Yes. The shared Sem 1-2 management core builds the management foundation regardless of background. Engineers entering from technical roles typically adapt well because operations management is analytically grounded. The specialisation content will be most immediately applicable to professionals with 2-5 years of plant or production floor experience. Freshers benefit more from the management foundation but the spec subjects will be more abstract without operational context.

faqs.question: Is this spec relevant for service operations management (hospitals, IT companies, logistics firms)?
faqs.answer: Production and Operations Management has historically been manufacturing-focused. However, operations management principles — capacity planning, quality management, process improvement, supply chain — apply to service operations as well. IT operations managers, hospital operations professionals, and logistics managers can extract relevant content. For pure service operations roles, verify that the specific Sem 3-4 subjects address your sector before enrolling.

faqs.question: Does this MBA help qualify for government manufacturing sector roles (PSUs)?
faqs.answer: Yes, provided the specific notification accepts UGC-DEB online degrees. Central government PSU recruitment for management roles has increasingly accepted UGC-DEB online degrees. Production and Operations Management is specifically relevant for manufacturing PSU roles in BHEL, BEML, HAL, and similar organisations. Always verify the specific notification's eligibility criteria before applying.

faqs.question: What is the salary range for Operations Management MBA graduates?
faqs.answer: Plant managers and operations heads with 5-10 years of manufacturing experience who complete this MBA typically see packages of Rs 14-28 lakh. VP Operations and COO roles at mid-size manufacturing companies pay Rs 22-45 lakh. The degree most significantly impacts earning potential when it qualifies a technical specialist for the formal management track that their company's HR policy requires for senior operations leadership roles.

faqs.question: Can I pursue this MBA alongside a Six Sigma certification?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pursuing Six Sigma Green Belt or Black Belt alongside this MBA is a common strategy for quality and operations professionals. The MBA provides the management and strategic context; the Six Sigma certification validates process improvement methodology proficiency. Together, they create a strong credentials combination for quality head and operations director roles at manufacturing companies that require both management qualifications and quality certifications.

chooseIf: You are a production engineer, plant manager, or operations professional in manufacturing and need a formal management credential covering supply chain, quality, and production planning.
You are targeting Plant Director, VP Operations, or COO roles at manufacturing companies that require a management degree alongside operational experience.
You want a dedicated manufacturing operations MBA at a NIRF #22 university under Rs 2 lakh.
consider: You need cross-functional management breadth beyond operations — General Management covers operations planning alongside finance, HR, and marketing.

You are targeting supply chain management as your primary focus — check whether an SCM-specific certification (APICS CPIM, CSCP) better meets your target employer's requirements alongside this degree.
You are in service operations (IT, hospitality, healthcare) rather than manufacturing — verify spec content applicability to service sector operations before enrolling.
